properties.description: Raised in 2009, this pole was created to honour Rose Cole Yelton, her family and all those who lived in Stanley Park. This pole is erected in front of the house site where the Cole family lived until 1935. Until the time of her passing in 2002...

properties.description: This totem pole commemorating the Canada Centennial in 1967 stands on the grounds of the Municipal Centre. The totem pole was carved by John Edward ("Ted") Neel, a member of a noted family of carvers. This pole is one of four in the area, involving a comm
properties.name: Surrey Columbian Centennial Totem Pole

properties.description: Totem pole carved by Don Colp

properties.name: Prestonpans Totem Pole

properties.description: Ga'akstalas, carved by Wayne Alfred and Beau Dick in1991, is based on a design by Russel Smith. The pole depicts many important figures in Kwakwada'wakw culture. Red Cedar-bark Man is an ancestor who survived the great flood and gave the people...
properties.name: Ga'akstalas

properties.description: Carved house posts are used in traditional First Nations cedar houses to support the huge roof beams. This pole is a replica of a house post carved by Kwakwaka'wakw artist Charlie James in the early 1900s. Tony Hunt carved this replica in 1987...
properties.name: Thunderbird House Post

properties.description: First nations origin stories tell of the animals and supernatural beings who helped found family lineages. These stories are celebrated in songs, dances, and totem pole carvings. Kwakwaka'wakw artist Oscar Maltipi carved this pole in 1968...
properties.name: Oscar Maltipi

properties.description: In kwakwaka'wakw (kwakiutl) ceremonies, carved staffs called talking sticks are held by people making important speeches on behalf of a chief. This pole represents the talking stick and characters in an Owikeno story belonging to Chief Wakas...
properties.name: Chief Wakas

properties.description: Carved in 1987 by Nisga'a artist Norman Tait along with his son Isaac, brother Robert, and nephew Ron Telek, this pole depicts how the Tait family's Eagle clan adopted the beaver as their crest, and how the eagle and raven met and shared the sky...
properties.name: Beaver Crest

properties.description: Hesquiat artist Tim Paul and Ditidaht artist Art Thompson carved this pole in 1988 to represent important characters in Nuu-chah-nulth history.
properties.name: Sky Chief

properties.description: Based on a Squamish Nation legend, the giant two-headed serpent seen on this pole is said to have strong magical powers. According to the legend, a young man from Sta7msh, the first village along the Squamish River, was sent on a quest to slay the serpent

properties.description: Evergreen Park was built as part of a beautification effort in 2007. It has several sculptures and memorials dedicated to the local Native American tribes in the Rogue Valley, including a totem pole and several bear sculptures and a memorial plaque
properties.name: Evergreen Park
